figures.caption: Figure 1: EMDG Monte Carlo (N=50): mean entropy and patient viability trajectories over a fixed horizon. Step corresponds to discrete tool-query iterations; trajectories are padded after stopping by carrying forward the terminal state, ensuring step-wise consistency (e.g., time cannot decrease).

Figure 2: EMDG ablations (N=50): terminal patient viability and terminal deliberation time across controller variants. Bars show means and error bars denote ±1σ over seeds. The full triadic controller achieves the highest viability while minimizing deliberation time, illustrating the necessity of jointly pricing space, time, and enforcing an explicit stopping boundary.
Figure 3: Triadic Cognitive Architecture (TCA): a coupled loop between Space (state), Time (dynamics), and Epistemic Truth (belief revision).
